name: HumanMetagenomeDB

relevance.justification: Contains 69,822 human metagenome samples with species-level microbiome data (gut, oral, skin, vagina, lung, etc.), 58 distinct disease/phenotype diagnoses, and per-sample demographic attributes including sex, age, BMI, and ethnicity. Structured in 203 standardized metadata fields with CSV export. Loses 1 point because active maintenance is uncertain (last confirmed release v1.1 June 2020, ~5+ years ago) and disease coding uses free-text diagnoses rather than standard ontology IDs (MONDO, ICD).

novelty.justification: No existing BioThings API covers human metagenome sample metadata at this scale or entity type. MyGene/MyChem/MyVariant/MyDisease do not contain metagenome run-level records with linked demographics, phenotypes, geographic origin, and sequencing QC. The 69,822 samples from 580 independent studies with 203 standardized attributes represent a clearly novel entity class (human microbiome sample + demographic + phenotype + sequencing metadata) not yet represented in any BioThings endpoint.

openness.justification: Published under Creative Commons Attribution License (CC-BY), permitting unrestricted reuse, distribution, and reproduction with attribution. No login required for web interface or CSV export. Python bulk-download script publicly available on GitHub (mdsufz/hmgdb_script) with no API key requirement. Minor limitation: no formal bulk dump URL on the canonical homepage; data accessed via filtered CSV exports or the GitHub downloader script.

evidence.scope: Curated, standardized metadata for 69,822 human metagenomes from SRA (95.4%) and MG-RAST. Covers 580 independent studies across 58 countries. Metadata includes 203 attributes spanning host demographics (age, sex, BMI, ethnicity), health/disease (58 diagnoses), sampling site (gut, oral, skin, vagina, lung, bone, biofluids), geographic coordinates, and sequencing QC metrics.
evidence.overlap: No meaningful overlap with existing BioThings APIs — this is a sample/run-level microbiome metadata resource, not a gene/variant/drug/disease ontology resource. SRA and MG-RAST are data repositories, not BioThings-indexed knowledge graphs.

api_info.record_coverage: CSV export via web interface; bulk download via GitHub Python script (mdsufz/hmgdb_script)

risks: Last confirmed release was v1.1 in June 2020 (~6 years ago at eval time); 'twice-yearly update' cadence may have lapsed — verify current maintenance status before ingestion
No bulk flat-file download on canonical homepage; data acquired via filtered CSV exports or GitHub script, which may not expose the full 203-attribute schema in one download
Disease/phenotype annotations are free-text diagnoses, not mapped to MONDO/ICD/HPO — normalization step required before BioThings integration
Aspera protocol in downloader script requires ascp binary and SSH key; standard HTTPS path should be verified as still functional
next_actions: Run site inspection to confirm current database version, update date, and bulk CSV download feasibility
Check whether full 203-attribute metadata is exportable in a single CSV or requires per-study downloads
Map free-text disease annotations to MONDO/HPO identifiers as part of plugin transformation
Verify GitHub script (mdsufz/hmgdb_script) still works and covers full dataset
